background: A jury having heard the evidence in a criminal case, is debating whether to render a verdict of guilty or not guilty. Because it is worse to convict an innocent person of a crime they did not commit than let a guilty person go free, each case starts with a presumption of innocence. This is analogous to the presumption that $H_0$ is true in a statistical hypothesis test. Other analogies exist.

task.query: To make these connections, fill in the cells of the following table with the labels: (a) Acquittal of an innocent person; (b) Conviction of an innocent person; (c) Acquittal of a guilty person; (d) Declaring a criminal guilty.
task.answer: \begin{tabular}{r|c|c|}\multicolumn{2}{c}{ TRUTH } \DECISION OF JURY & \multicolumn{1}{c|}{ Did not do crime } & Did crime \Not Guilty & (a) & (c) \Guilty & (b) & (d) \\ \hline\end{tabular}

task.query: Which type of decision is analogous to a type I error?
task.answer: Declaring an innocent person guilty (b) is analogous to a type I error.

task.query: Which is analogous to a type II error?
task.answer: Declaring a criminal not guilty (c) is analogous to a type II error.
